what_it_does: Renders a standalone preview card for the brand's bronze line-icon library, showing descriptive copy, a centered image asset, and compact metadata for color, stroke, caps, fill, and file location.
observations: Loads a shared stylesheet named _card.css and adds page-local CSS for the card, lead text, image sheet, and metadata row.
The lead copy identifies the library as bronze line icons using color #988058.
The copy states the library has approximately 80 glyphs covering people, files, processes, building types, devices, and platform actions.
The image preview references ../assets/icons/icon-library-bronze.jpg with alt text "Bronze icon library".
The metadata row lists color #988058, stroke ~1.5 px, round caps, no fill, and assets/icons/icon-library-bronze.jpg.
standouts: The file combines visual preview, usage context, and technical icon styling metadata in one small card.
The asset is displayed as a centered contained image inside a surface sheet.
The metadata presents icon construction properties as concise labeled values.
conventions: Uses a compact standalone HTML document for a preview surface.
Keeps layout styling in simple class selectors local to the file while depending on shared card tokens from _card.css.
Uses CSS custom properties for typography, color, radius, and surface background.
Uses bold inline values to emphasize key design metadata.
concerns: The HTML is written in a compressed single-line structure, which reduces edit readability.
The displayed file path omits the ../ prefix used by the actual img src, which may require context to reconcile.
Icon count and stroke width are approximate text values rather than structured data.
notes: This file is a design-system preview card documenting and displaying the bronze line-icon library asset.
questions: Is the displayed file path intended to be documentation-relative while the img src is page-relative?
Should the approximate glyph count and stroke width be treated as stable design metadata or descriptive copy?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual behavior: it presents the bronze icon library and its usage/style metadata.
intention_signals: It appears intended to brief viewers on what the bronze icon library is, where it is used, what it looks like, and the core styling attributes needed to apply it consistently.
novelty: The page treats an icon library image as a documented design-system substrate with usage context and construction metadata in the same preview.
contribution: Contributes a file-local pattern for preserving icon-library identity, visual reference, usage contexts, and core stroke/color metadata as a reusable design-system ledger entry.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML preview card for a bronze brand line-icon library image, with descriptive usage copy and metadata for color, stroke, caps, fill, and asset path.
intention_for_embedding: This was for documenting and visually presenting the brand's bronze icon library as a design-system reference asset.
